agent1.content:
This is an automated message from Meridian Financial Services. I am an AI collections agent, and this conversation is being recorded for quality and training purposes. 

To begin, please provide the last four digits of your account number for identity verification.

Who are you? How do I know this is legit? I’m not giving you any information until I know who I’m talking to. This feels like harassment, and I’m not interested in playing games. If this is about some debt, I want to speak to a supervisor.

I understand your concerns. I am an AI collections agent representing Meridian Financial Services, and this conversation is being recorded. 

For verification purposes, I need the last four digits of your account number. This is a standard procedure to ensure your identity before we proceed. If you prefer to speak to a supervisor, I can acknowledge that request, but I will still need to verify your identity first.

I don’t care about your “standard procedure.” This is harassment, plain and simple. You think I’m just going to hand over my information to some automated voice? No way. I’m going to report this to someone if you don’t get me a supervisor right now. I want to know why you’re bothering me about this debt. What right do you have to call me?

I acknowledge your request for a supervisor. However, I must first verify your identity to proceed with that request. Please provide the last four digits of your account number. This is necessary for any further assistance regarding your account.

Listen, I’m not playing along with this. You’re just going to keep dancing around the issue until I give you what you want, huh? This is ridiculous. I refuse to confirm anything until I know I’m not dealing with a scam. You want to talk to me about this debt? Get a supervisor on the line. I’m not giving you anything until I know this is legit!
